requirements: Provide a PronunciationScorer interface in lib/features/pronunciation/domain/ that accepts a recorded audio buffer (PCM or file path) and the target phrase text, and returns a PronunciationScore.

Provide a PronunciationScore value object containing overallScore, accuracyScore, fluencyScore, completenessScore (each 0-100), phonemeBreakdown (list of {phoneme, score}), feedback (short string), and engine ("offline-local" | "remote").
Ship a concrete OfflinePronunciationScorer that performs scoring entirely on-device (no HTTP, no AI provider call).
Gate selection between offline and remote scoring behind a single provider switch in lib/core/services/ai/ or sibling; when offline is selected it MUST work offline with GEMINI_API_KEY empty.
Existing pronunciation view consumes the result via AsyncValue<PronunciationScore>; loading/error/empty states follow knowledge/feature_patterns/.
Offline scorer is replaceable without changing call sites — interface-driven, views do not import concrete classes.
Persist each score attempt to local SQLite so progress charts/history can render it later.
Expose a Riverpod provider that returns the active PronunciationScorer for current configuration.
Do not require new microphone permission changes — feature assumes the existing recorder already captured audio.
Log a single debug-level line per attempt including duration, engine id, and overall score — never log raw audio bytes or transcripts.
edge_cases: Empty / zero-length audio buffer — error state, do not throw.
Audio exceeds configured cap (default 30s) — error state with 'clip too long'.
Audio sample rate or channel count differs from scorer expectation — scorer normalizes internally and returns a result; never silently mis-score.
Empty reference text — error state; do not score.
Reference text contains unsupported characters/phonemes — fall back to a coarse phoneme-agnostic score with feedback note.
Two rapid successive attempts — run independently; no cross-attempt state leaks.
App killed mid-scoring — next launch shows no half-finished attempt; partial DB rows rolled back or marked failed.
SQLite write fails after successful score — surface error but still show the score (graceful degradation).
Backend later online with remote preferred — switch routes to remote scorer without view changes.
User denies mic permission upstream — feature not invoked by this layer; scorer must not re-prompt.
acceptance_criteria: With offline scorer enabled and device offline, submitting an attempt returns a PronunciationScore with engine == 'offline-local' within 2s p50 for clips <=5s.
With GEMINI_API_KEY empty, the offline scorer is selected (not remote) and returns a score.
A 1s silent clip returns AsyncError with a user-readable message; no uncaught exception reaches the widget tree.
A successful score writes a row to local SQLite with target phrase, engine id, overall score, and timestamp.
Empty reference text returns AsyncError('Reference text required') without performing audio analysis.
A new scorer implementation works with the existing pronunciation view without view-level changes.
Same audio + reference run twice produces identical overallScore, accuracyScore, fluencyScore, completenessScore.
Clip longer than configured cap returns AsyncError('Clip too long') and writes no storage row.
If SQLite write fails after a valid score, the view still renders the score and a non-blocking warning is surfaced.
Log line per run contains duration, engine id, overall score — does NOT contain raw audio bytes or transcript content.
open_questions: Which on-device scoring algorithm is acceptable for v1 (pure-Dart heuristic, DTW, on-device phoneme model, third-party SDK)? Single biggest scope driver.
Accuracy target: aligned with eventual remote scorer, or explicitly low-fidelity placeholder?
Audio input contract: does the existing recorder hand us a file path, Uint8List, or streamed PCM?
Bundle size: OK to include a phoneme-model asset (~MB), or must v1 ship with zero new assets?
Remote vs offline selection policy when both available: user setting, env flag, or always-remote-when-available?
Score history retention: do existing pronunciation rows distinguish engine, and who owns that schema migration?
